title: /Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/.claude 内容を表示せよ
summary_text: **Top 3 Drift Risks** - **Runtime procedure is duplicated across adapter and canonical prompt (highest risk).** [AGENTS.md](/Users/masayuki/AGENTS.md):55 says detailed slash-command runtime must live in canonical prompt files, but [CODEX.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/CODEX.md):47 still carries procedural `/kernel` and `/vote` behavior that overlaps with [kernel.md](/Users/masayuki/.codex/prompts/kernel.md):31 and [kernel.md](/Users/masayuki/.codex/prompts/kernel.md):52. This is the main drift vector. - **Approval-boundary taxonomy is duplicated with subtle expansion.** Global boundary list in [AGENTS.md](/Users/masayuki/AGENTS.md):14 differs from adapter/prompt wording in [CODEX.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/CODEX.md):54 and [kernel.md](/Users/masayuki/.codex/prompts/kernel.md):58 (`large system changes` appears outside the canonical global list). This can cause inconsistent `/vote` triggering over time. - **Autonomy vs clarification behavior can drift by wording, not logic.** [AGENTS.md](/Users/masayuki/AGENTS.md):13 and [AGENTS.md](/Users/masayuki/AGENTS.md):29 push autonomous progress with minimal questioning, while [kernel.md](/Users/masayuki/.codex/prompts/kernel.md):34 adds an explicit “ask-user-input-style clarification round … and pause.” It is conditionally scoped (“if material ambiguity remains”), but this wording is fragile and easy to over-apply. **Single Best Minimal Fix** - **Thin `CODEX.md` further by removing procedural `/kernel`/`/vote` behavior and keeping only invariants + pointer to canonical prompts.** Concretely, collapse [CODEX.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/CODEX.md):47-57 into a short “runtime behavior is authoritative in `~/.codex/prompts/kernel.md` and `~/.codex/prompts/vote.md`; adapter defines boundaries only.” This single change eliminates the biggest duplication surface and reduces both approval-taxonomy and autonomy-wording drift.
